game_info.title: The Past Within - Cooperative Puzzle Adventure
game_info.concept: **The Past Within:** "The Past Within" is a cooperative point-and-click puzzle adventure requiring two players, each on their own device, to communicate externally (e.g., voice chat) to solve interconnected mysteries. One player navigates a 2D environment representing "The Past," while the other explores a 3D environment in "The Future," both interacting with objects by tapping them to examine, collect, or manipulate. Core gameplay involves players describing visual cues, item properties, or puzzle states from their unique perspective to their partner, who then uses that information to unlock mechanisms or progress puzzles in their own dimension. The game features 2 chapters, with progression achieved by collaboratively solving all puzzles within a chapter, and offers replayability by allowing players to swap roles and experience alternate puzzle solutions. There are no failure states beyond being unable to solve a puzzle; the game concludes upon the successful completion of all cooperative objectives across both timelines.
game_info.description: "The Past Within" is a cooperative puzzle adventure where two players collaborate across time. One player explores a 2D Past environment while another navigates a 3D Future environment. Players must communicate externally to share clues, solve interconnected puzzles, and progress through 2 chapters. Click on objects to examine or interact with them, and use information from your partner to unlock mechanisms in your timeline. Complete all puzzles in both chapters to win!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move cursor/selection in menus and navigate between objects
Space: Interact with selected object (examine, collect, use)
Z: Use held item on selected object
Shift: Switch between Past and Future perspectives (single-player mode)
Enter: Start game
Esc: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart to main menu

TEST_1.test_description: Basic movement and interaction test. Validates that the player can navigate through the game interface, select objects, and perform basic interactions without errors.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Cycle through available objects in both timelines, attempt interactions with each object, and verify that the game responds appropriately to all inputs.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Successfully interact with multiple objects, collect items, and verify game state updates correctly. No crashes or stuck states.

TEST_1.expected: Successfully interact with multiple objects, collect items, and verify game state updates correctly. No crashes or stuck states.

TEST_2.test_description: Win condition test - optimal puzzle solving. Tests the complete game flow by solving all puzzles in the correct sequence to achieve victory.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Follow the optimal puzzle solution path: collect key items in Past, decode symbols in Future, use combined information to unlock mechanisms, and complete both chapters in sequence.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Successfully complete Chapter 1 and Chapter 2 puzzles, reach GAME_OVER_WIN state, and verify all puzzle dependencies are satisfied correctly.

TEST_2.expected: Successfully complete Chapter 1 and Chapter 2 puzzles, reach GAME_OVER_WIN state, and verify all puzzle dependencies are satisfied correctly.

TEST_3.test_description: Timeline synchronization test. Validates that actions in one timeline correctly affect the other timeline's puzzle state.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Perform actions in Past timeline and verify corresponding changes appear in Future timeline. Test puzzle dependencies between timelines.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Timeline states remain synchronized, puzzle hints from one timeline unlock solutions in the other, no desynchronization errors occur.

TEST_3.strategy: Perform actions in Past timeline and verify corresponding changes appear in Future timeline. Test puzzle dependencies between timelines.
TEST_3.expected: Timeline states remain synchronized, puzzle hints from one timeline unlock solutions in the other, no desynchronization errors occur.
